articles.title: Coal India To Britannia: Bengal Election Results Trigger Rally In Key Stocks
articles.description: Election-linked momentum is already visible in several West Bengal-focused stocks, says Jatin Gedia, with the uptrend expected to continue in the near term. Stocks like CESC have been rallying over the past few weeks, reflecting sustained buying interest and positive sentiment. Across sectors, FMCG, banking and infrastructure names are also gaining traction. Emami and Bandhan Bank are showing strength on charts with potential for further upside. However, caution is advised in railway stocks like Titagarh Rail Systems, which may see some consolidation after recent gains. The broader strategy, according to Gedia, is to ride the momentum with trailing stop losses while avoiding overbought counters.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee accuses BJP of harassing workers; assures win, asks not to fear
articles.description: Greetings to everyone. I urge the counting agents and candidates not to leave their assigned areas. Please do not leave the strong rooms where the vote counting is underway. This, I believe, was part of the BJP’s plan. I have been saying since yesterday that they will show their leads in the first two to three rounds, and our results will be reflected later. In several areas, after two to three rounds, vote counting at nearly 100 locations was stopped, and seven EVMs were reportedly found red-handed in Kalyani that were not identical. This appears to be a deliberate effort by the Central Government using central forces. They are allegedly attacking TMC workers everywhere, breaking offices, and forcibly taking control. They have been accused of manipulating votes in the name of SIR to win seats. However, there are at least 70 to 100 seats where we are leading, but those results are not being shown. This is completely false information being circulated. Earlier, such reports were being shared by DIC, but that changed yesterday. The Election Commission is acting according to its own decisions, with the support of central forces. Even our police are allegedly working under pressure, with their heads down. That is why I am telling counting agents and party workers not to lose heart. I have said before that we will win by sunset. Only three to four rounds have been completed so far, and the process goes up to 16 to 18 rounds. Wait and watch. We are with you—do not be afraid.

articles.title: After 7 H-1B rejections, Microsoft techie instead gets US Green Card: Her 5 lessons she learnt after years of long wait
articles.description: An Indian-origin software engineer at Microsoft has gone viral after sharing how she finally secured a US Green Card following seven consecutive rejections in the H-1B visa lottery between 2019 and 2025. In her post, she opened up about the emotional toll of repeated setbacks, describing it as a gradual loss of confidence rather than a single moment of failure. She later moved to Canada, returned to the US on an L-1 visa, and eventually received her Green Card through the EB-1 category in 2025.

articles.title: State’s rabies fight falters as survey exposes widespread vaccination gaps
articles.description: Goa's rabies control efforts show significant shortcomings, with a third of pet owners neglecting vaccinations and sterilization rates alarmingly low. A joint survey revealed poor access to vet services, widespread myths, and a lack of effective bite reporting. Preventable rabies deaths remain a real risk without immediate, targeted interventions and community engagement.

articles.title: Kerala Polls: Explosives seized in CM Vijayan's constituency
articles.description: Police found explosive materials including country-made bombs and gunpowder in Dharmadam. This constituency is represented by Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan. The seizure occurred a day before vote counting for the Kerala assembly elections. Officials acted on a tip about illegal firecracker manufacturing. A case has been registered against a local resident. Meanwhile, Chief Minister Vijayan updated his social media bio.

articles.title: In past 8 yrs, 1.2k minors were sexually abused, and 13% got pregnant: Goa VAU
articles.description: Shocking data from Goa's victim assistance unit reveals 13% of sexually abused minor girls became pregnant over eight years. Teen pregnancies are most common among 16-18 year olds. Experts stress early, consistent education from trusted adults, starting in Class V, to combat this alarming trend and protect vulnerable youth.
